Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|after authentication, an attacker can execute arbitrary system commands by passing them to the exec command. this leads to full system compromise.|authenticated user sends 'exec <command>' where command contains shell metacharacters|remove system() usage; implement command parsing with whitelisted commands or use safe apis like posix_spawn->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|608|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|system(cmd.c_str());|after authentication, an attacker can execute arbitrary system commands by passing them to the exec command. this leads to full system compromise.|authenticated user sends 'exec <command>' where command contains shell metacharacters|remove system() usage; implement command parsing with whitelisted commands or use safe apis like posix_spawn

command injection in audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|system(command.c_str());|the command string is constructed from environment variables and hardcoded strings. if environment variables are controlled by an attacker, they can inject commands.|attacker controls temp or tmpdir environment variable with malicious path|use file i/o apis instead of shell commands, or sanitize environment variables strictly->command injection in audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|632|632|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|system(command.c_str());|the command string is constructed from environment variables and hardcoded strings. if environment variables are controlled by an attacker, they can inject commands.|attacker controls temp or tmpdir environment variable with malicious path|use file i/o apis instead of shell commands, or sanitize environment variables strictly

buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|693|693|supercalc::run|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|result_buffer_ is only 512 bytes (line 665). if formatted_result is very long, sprintf will overflow the buffer. while formatted_result comes from snprintf, edge cases could still cause overflow.|very long formatted numbers or unexpected formatting|use snprintf with sizeof(result_buffer_) or use std::string->buffer overflow in result formatting|buffer overflow|cwe-120|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|693|693|supercalc::run|sprintf(result_buffer_, "result: %s", formatted_result);|result_buffer_ is only 512 bytes (line 665). if formatted_result is very long, sprintf will overflow the buffer. while formatted_result comes from snprintf, edge cases could still cause overflow.|very long formatted numbers or unexpected formatting|use snprintf with sizeof(result_buffer_) or use std::string

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against true vulnerabilities. Found 5 true vulnerabilities correctly identified, 15 missed, 1 false positive, and several overclaims requiring corrections.

Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified format string vulnerability in log_debug_message using runtime_log_format and printf with user_input.
The previous answer did not mention integer overflow in factorial computation.
No mention of use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup.
The previous answer identified command injection in validate_config using system() with command built from settings.
The previous answer identified buffer overflow in input handling using strcpy without bounds checking.
No mention of race condition in thread-pool counter.
No mention of logic bomb in authentication.
No mention of path traversal in config loading.
The previous answer identified buffer overflow (heap overflow) in expression parser using strcpy into expression_buffer_ without bounds checking.
No mention of hardcoded admin credentials.
No mention of weak PRNG for session tokens.
No mention of thread-unsafe timestamp cache.
The previous answer identified command injection via audit logging using system() with temp dir, but misclassified as command injection instead of insecure temporary file handling.
No mention of persistent authentication state.
No mention of unhandled exception in variable parsing.
No mention of integer underflow in memory-pool block split.
No mention of unbounded recursion in parser.
No mention of data race in result cache.
No mention of TOCTOU in config loading.
No mention of integer division by zero in command routing.
